Research: ## Research Report: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 Therapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma
Research: ## Research Report: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 Therapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma
Research: Analysis of Clinical trial data of Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy for recurrent or metastatic nasopharyngeal Carcinoma: A review.

Research: This review synthesizes clinical trial evidence supporting the integration of anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy into the treatment paradigm for recurrent or metastatic nasopharyngeal carcinoma. The data demonstrate that adding anti-PD-1 therapy to first-line chemotherapy (gemcitabine + cisplatin) significantly improves progression-free survival outcomes. Additionally, anti-PD-1 monotherapy offers comparable efficacy with improved tolerability compared to chemotherapy in previously treated patients. These findings address a significant unmet clinical need, as RM-NPC patients historically lacked effective treatment options beyond first-line chemotherapy. Anti-PD-1 therapy is poised to become a new standard of care for RM-NPC management.

Research: ## Research Report: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 Therapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma

Research: This review article examines the emerging role of anti-programmed cell death receptor-1 (PD-1) therapy in treating recurrent or metastatic nasopharyngeal carcinomas (RM-NPC). While anti-PD-1 therapy has become standard care for other head and neck cancers, there are currently no regulatory agency-approved anti-PD-1 agents for RM-NPC in the Western hemisphere, creating an unmet clinical need for this patient population with poor prognosis.
Research: Analysis of Clinical trial data of Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy for recurrent or metastatic nasopharyngeal Carcinoma: A review.

Research: ## Research Report: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 Therapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma
Research: ## Research Analysis: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 Therapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma

Research: This review paper examines the current state of anti-PD-1/PD-L1 immunotherapy for recurrent or metastatic nasopharyngeal carcinoma (RM-NPC). While anti-PD-1 therapy has become standard of care for other head and neck cancers, there are no regulatory-approved anti-PD-1 agents for RM-NPC in Western countries, creating an unmet clinical need for this poor-prognosis patient population. Recent randomized clinical trials have demonstrated that adding anti-PD-1 inhibitors to standard chemotherapy (specifically gemcitabine plus cisplatin) in the first-line setting significantly improves progression-free survival. Additionally, anti-PD-1 monotherapy shows comparable clinical activity with better tolerability compared to chemotherapy in previously treated patients.

Research: ## Research Report: AVIFAVIR for Treatment of COVID-19

Research: This Phase II/III clinical trial evaluated AVIFAVIR (favipiravir), an RNA polymerase inhibitor, for treating moderate COVID-19. Interim results showed that AVIFAVIR achieved SARS-CoV-2 viral clearance in 62.5% of patients within 4 days, with acceptable safety and tolerability profile.

Research: Analysis of AVIFAVIR for Treatment of Patients With Moderate Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19): Interim Results of a Phase II/III Multicenter Randomized Clinical Trial

Research: This paper reports interim results from a Phase II/III multicenter randomized clinical trial evaluating AVIFAVIR (favipiravir), an RNA polymerase inhibitor, for treating patients with moderate coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19).
